prevalence.source: SBPT (Sociedade Brasileira de Pneumologia e Tisiologia) and DataSUS
prevalence.trend: stable with slight increase in urban areas due to air pollution

riskFactors: Air pollution in urban centers like São Paulo
Biomass fuel exposure in rural households
vulnerablePopulations: Rural women using biomass fuels for cooking
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: GOLD criteria for COPD, chronic cough/sputum for 3+ months/year for 2+ years

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited spirometry access, <20% in primary care
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 35% well-controlled with access to SUS medications

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: SBPT/GOLD guidelines, spirometry when available, clinical diagnosis common
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Bronchodilators (salbutamol, formoterol) via SUS, inhaled corticosteroids, smoking cessation programs
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Smoking prevalence, biomass fuel exposure in rural areas, limited spirometry access, air pollution in São Paulo/Rio
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: SUS provision of inhalers, national smoking cessation program, SBPT guidelines
